title: Helper differentiation and second route opportunity phase
summary: Lane 4 should give one non-route-marker helper a real in-field difference, then add a second tactile living-world opportunity seam on another route family.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the non-route-marker helper differentiation handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Helper differentiation still needs an in-field proof, not just a map proof.

execution_order.goal: Implement the non-route-marker helper differentiation pass.
execution_order.why_now: At least one other helper should change how a route feels in the field.

execution_order.goal: Review the non-route-marker helper differentiation pass.
execution_order.why_now: The helper row should become more meaningful without growing.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the second tactile route-opportunity handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The living-world opportunity system should prove itself on more than one route family.

execution_order.goal: Implement the second tactile route-opportunity seam.
execution_order.why_now: A second route proof will make the replay system feel intentional instead of one-off.

execution_order.goal: Review the second tactile route-opportunity seam.
execution_order.why_now: Replay should deepen route feel without confusing route identity.

guardrails: No new support slots, inventory, or crafting layer.
Prefer clue visibility, targeting, or route emphasis over longer copy.
If a helper still cannot be made distinct, simplify instead of expanding.
